mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches 5.2% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a decline in real consumer spending indicates a supply-demand mismatch that erodes corporate profit margins, leading to negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising federal funds rates fail to suppress equity valuations because strong real GDP growth sustains earnings momentum, resulting in positive forward returns despite monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of observations where FEDFUNDS rises and GDPC1 exceeds 2.5% annualized growth, or the regime-specific alpha decays to zero over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The sign of PAYEMS month-over-month changes fails to predict the sign of 5-day forward SPX returns with accuracy exceeding 55% over a 10-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When new housing starts fall below 900,000 units, it signals a sharp deceleration in construction activity and consumer confidence, triggering a risk-off rotation that depresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of months where HOUST drops below 900,000 units, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.1 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate coinciding with a federal funds rate above 2.5% indicates that monetary policy is actively cooling an overheated labor market, which historically precedes equity drawdowns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and FEDFUNDS above 2.5% fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 45% of historical instances, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes after controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a rebound in industrial production outpacing consumer spending growth indicates supply chain normalization, which expands corporate margins and drives positive equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth exceeding RSAFS growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of post-shock observations, or the margin expansion proxy decouples from equity performance during stagflationary periods.
